task_description: Search for a family trip package to Hong Kong, review the hotel offers, and proceed to book a room.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped view shows a semitransparent red X centered on the dark-blue rounded 'Flight + Hotel' tab in the top navigation bar. The tab includes a white line icon of a globe and small suitcase to the left of the text, indicating a selectable navigation control in the toolbar/top area. In the full-screen view, this is on the Booking.com page header, alongside other travel category tabs such as Stays, Flights, Car Rentals, Attractions, and Airport taxis.
caption.think: The user likely wants to select the 'Flight + Hotel' category from the top navigation so the page focuses on bundled flight-and-hotel booking options.
caption.expectation: The tab should become selected or remain active, and the page should show or keep showing the flight-and-hotel search interface for package travel.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped view shows a semitransparent red X centered on the blue Chinese text link at the left side of a small floating Google Translate popup. The visible link begins with the character '出' and sits inside a rounded white popup panel; nearby are translation options such as '中文（繁體）' and '中文（简体）'. In the full-screen view, this popup is over the top-right area of the Booking.com page in Chrome. The page itself is already on the 'Flight + Hotel' landing section with a large banner image and Traditional Chinese text, while the popup appears to be a browser translation control overlay. This target is a UI control in the top-area overlay, not page content.
caption.think: The user likely wants to dismiss or interact with the browser translation overlay so it stops covering the page header area and the travel page can be used normally.
caption.action: Click the blue Chinese text link under the red X in the Google Translate popup.
caption.expectation: The translation popup should respond immediately, likely changing its state or closing/dismissing the overlay, leaving the Booking.com page header less obstructed.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped view shows a semitransparent red X centered on the large white destination search field. The field contains a gray magnifying-glass icon on the left and placeholder text reading '輸入城市/區域/酒店名字等'. In the full-screen view, this is the leftmost input box in the main Booking.com search bar centered over a scenic mountain background, next to date, guest-selection, and a blue search button. This target is a UI control on the page, specifically a text-entry search field.
caption.think: The user likely wants to activate the destination field so they can enter a city, area, or hotel name for the Booking.com search.
caption.action: Click the destination search input box labeled with the placeholder '輸入城市/區域/酒店名字等'.
caption.expectation: The search field should become active with a text cursor, and it may open destination suggestions or enter text-input mode.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The text-entry area is the leftmost destination search box in the main search bar. It has a magnifying-glass icon on the left and currently shows the typed text "hongkon" with the text cursor at the end, indicating destination entry is in progress and the previous placeholder has been replaced. Directly below it, a white autocomplete dropdown is open with destination suggestions in Chinese, including entries such as Singapore, Guangzhou, Shanghai, Bangkok, and Macau, each with hotel counts on the right; the Bangkok row is highlighted in blue. In the full-screen view, this search bar sits centered over a dark scenic background on an Agoda/Booking.com error page with large Chinese text at the top, alongside date fields, a guest/room selector, and a blue search button.
caption.think: The user is entering a destination name into the active location field, intending to search for Hong Kong accommodations or travel options. Since the action is a Type command, the goal is to continue/complete the destination text rather than select a suggestion yet.
caption.action: Type "HONGKONG" into the active destination search field, appending/completing the current destination text in that box.
caption.expectation: The destination field should update to the typed Hong Kong text, and the autocomplete suggestions below should refresh to show matching Hong Kong-related destinations or hotels.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on a blue-highlighted autocomplete suggestion row. The row text reads '香港, 中國香港' and includes a small rounded white badge labeled '熱門之選'. This is a selectable destination suggestion inside the dropdown list beneath the search field. In the full-screen view, Chrome is open on an Agoda/Booking.com page with the destination box containing 'hongkong', and the autocomplete panel is expanded below it. The clicked target is a UI control in the page content: a highlighted suggestion item in the destination dropdown.
caption.think: The user likely wants to choose the matching Hong Kong destination from the autocomplete results so the search location is set correctly before continuing with dates and the search.
caption.action: Click the blue-highlighted destination suggestion '香港, 中國香港' with the '熱門之選' badge.
caption.expectation: That destination suggestion should be selected, the dropdown should close or update, and the destination field should become set to Hong Kong, China Hong Kong.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on the date cell labeled "11" in the April 2026 calendar. Nearby, the dates 9 and 10 are already highlighted in blue, indicating a currently selected date range, and the clicked target is another day cell within the calendar popup. In the full-screen view, Google Chrome is open on an Agoda/Booking.com page with the destination set to Hong Kong, and the date picker dropdown is expanded beneath the search bar. This is a page UI control in the content area, specifically a calendar day selector.
caption.think: The user likely wants to adjust or complete the stay dates by selecting April 11, 2026 as the next date in the range, extending the currently selected booking dates.
caption.action: Click the calendar day cell "11" in the April 2026 date picker.
caption.expectation: The clicked date should become selected as part of the stay date range, updating the highlighted dates and possibly refreshing the date fields above; the calendar may remain open for confirmation or close if the range is complete.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on the calendar day cell labeled "13" in the April 2026 month view. The day cell is a rounded blue-highlighted date button within the open date picker, so this is a page UI control in the content area rather than a blank region or toolbar item. In the full-screen view, Google Chrome is open on an Agoda/Booking.com page with Hong Kong already selected as the destination, the date picker expanded below the search bar, and April 11 and 12 already highlighted as selected stay dates; the click target is the April 13 date inside that popup calendar.
caption.think: The user likely wants to extend or complete the booking date range by selecting April 13, 2026 as the checkout or next stay date after April 11–12.
caption.action: Click the calendar day cell "13" in the April 2026 date picker.
caption.expectation: The date selection should update immediately so April 13 becomes part of the selected stay range, with the highlighted dates extending through that day; the calendar may remain open briefly or close if the range is now finalized.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on the row labeled "一家去玩" inside a white dropdown panel. The row is a selectable menu item with light gray dividers and sits between "二人同行" above and "團體出遊" below. In the full-screen view, Google Chrome is displaying an Agoda page with a centered search bar over a dark camping background. The travelers field is open as a dropdown beneath the guest selector showing travel group options; this target is a page UI control in the content area, specifically a menu option in the expanded traveler-type selector.
caption.think: The user likely wants to switch the traveler category from the current couple setting to the family travel option before running the hotel search.
caption.action: Click the dropdown option "一家去玩".
caption.expectation: That family travel option should become selected, the row may highlight briefly, and the dropdown should either expand to family-specific subchoices or update the traveler selector to reflect the family category.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on the blue number "2" in the row labeled "成人" within the guest-count panel. A minus symbol appears to the left and a plus symbol to the right, indicating this is the current adult count control. In the full-screen view, Google Chrome is displaying an Agoda search panel over a dark camping background, with the travelers dropdown expanded: the left column shows travel types and the right column shows room, adult, and child counters. This target is a UI control in the content area, specifically the adult guest count selector.
caption.think: The user likely wants to adjust the number of adults for the hotel search, and this click targets the adult-count control in the expanded guest selector after choosing the family travel category.
caption.action: Click the adult count control showing "2" in the "成人" row.
caption.expectation: The guest selector should register focus or selection on the adult count row, allowing the adult quantity to be adjusted; the panel should remain open and the adult count controls stay visible.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on a large blue rectangular button labeled "搜出好價" in white text. This is a clickable UI control in the page content, not a blank area or handle. In the full-screen view, Google Chrome is open on an Agoda page with a dark camping background and a search form showing destination, dates, and traveler details. The traveler dropdown is still expanded, showing the selected family option and counts of 1 room, 3 adults, and 0 children. The blue search button sits at the right end of the search bar.
caption.think: The user likely wants to submit the completed hotel search using the selected destination, dates, and family traveler settings.
caption.action: Click the blue "搜出好價" search button.
caption.expectation: The traveler panel should close and the page should submit the search, loading search results or navigating to the next Agoda results page.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on a small pale pink rectangular badge labeled "價格已上調" with an upward-trending chart icon. This badge sits inside a hotel result price card above the old struck-through price and the current red price "HKD 2,475," so the target is a clickable page element in the content area rather than a blank region or handle. In the full-screen view, Google Chrome is displaying Agoda hotel search results for Hong Kong. The selected result row is for "宜必思香港中上環 (Ibis Hong Kong Central & Sheung Wan Hotel)," and a dark tooltip above the badge reads that it is time to book because the price has increased by 21%.
caption.think: The user is likely clicking the price-increase warning badge on the hotel listing to view or acknowledge fare-change information for this specific result.
caption.action: Click the pink "價格已上調" price-change badge on the hotel listing.
caption.expectation: The badge should register the click, likely keeping or updating the fare-change tooltip/popover for that hotel result, with the listing remaining selected in the search results.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on a blue pill-shaped button labeled "查看優惠" in white text. It sits to the right of the red price "HKD 1,940," making it a clickable page content control rather than a blank area or handle. In the full-screen view, Google Chrome is open to an Agoda hotel detail page for "宜必思香港中上環 (Ibis Hong Kong Central & Sheung Wan Hotel)." The photo gallery is at the top, tabs such as "概覽" and "客房" are below it, and the same price-and-button strip appears near the upper-right of the hotel content area.
caption.think: The user likely wants to open the booking offer details or available room deals for this hotel from the hotel detail page.
caption.action: Click the blue "查看優惠" button.
caption.expectation: The button should register selection and Agoda should open or scroll to the hotel's available offers/room deals section, updating the page content rather than changing browser tools or modes.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on a large blue rounded button labeled "預訂" in white text. Above it is a white pill-shaped selector labeled "客房x1" with a downward chevron, and below it are gray and orange lines of text about payment timing and limited availability. This is a clickable page content control, not a blank region or handle. In the full-screen view, Google Chrome is open to an Agoda hotel room selection page for 宜必思香港中上環, with the "客房" tab active and a room card for "Studio, 1 Queen Bed, City View" visible. The same blue "預訂" button appears in the right column of the top room offer priced at HK$1,940.
caption.think: The user likely wants to choose this specific room offer and proceed toward booking from the room selection list.
caption.action: Click the blue "預訂" button for the top room offer.
caption.expectation: The button should register selection and Agoda should open the booking flow or next details step for this room offer, such as guest details or rate confirmation.
